283e1c05-5238-4238-bb7f-289e39f85e87.exp: human papillomavirus (HPV). Most HPV infections cause no symptoms and resolve spontaneously. In some, they persist and result in was or precancerous lesions. The precancerous lesions increase the risk of cancer of the cervix, vulva, vagina, penis, anus, mouth, or throat. Nearly all cervical cancer is due to HPV with two types, HPV16 and HPV18, accounting for 70% of cases. Between 60 and 90% of the other cancers are also linked to HPV. HPV6 and HPV11 are common causes of genital was and respiratory papillomatosis. HPV infection is caused by a human papillomavirus, a DNA virus from the papillomavirus family, of which over 150 types are known. More than 40 types are transmitted through sexual contact and infect the anus and genitals. Risk factors for persistent HPV infections include early age of first sexual intercourse, multiple paners, smoking, and poor immune function. HPV is typically spread by sustained direct skin-to-skin contact with vaginal and anal sex being the most common methods. Occasionally, it can spread from a mother to her baby during pregnancy. It does not spread common items like toilet seats. People can become infected with more than one type of HPV. HPV only affects humans.

The trapezoid body is located in the caudal pons, or more specifically the pontine tegmentum.
The trapezoid body is part of the auditory pathway where some of the axons coming from the cochlear nucleus decussate, or cross over to the other side before traveling on to the superior olivary nucleus. This is believed to help with localization of sound.

c93484b9-8a4b-4ec1-90d1-7dff0ae4d035.exp: Ans. is 'a' i.e., Median Umbilical ligament Urachus* Allantois is a vestigial structure and initially bladder is continuous with it. Most of the allantois soon constricts to form a thick fibrous cord called the urachus, which extends from the apex of bladder to the umbilicus. In the adults, the median umbilical ligament represents the urachus. Incomplete closure of urachus results in urachal cyst, sinus or fistula.i) Urachal cyst: Middle part of allantois remains patent (proximal and distal parts are obliterated).ii) Urachal sinus: Patent inferior end of urachus dilate to form urachal sinus.iii) Urachal fistula : Entire urachus remains patent.

808d8da4-265f-43fb-aa60-4e4149933729.exp: TI-RADS is a repoing system for thyroid nodules on ultrasound proposed by American College of Radiology. It is based on:1. Composition (cystic-benign/solid-malignant)2. Echogenic foci(macro-calcifications - benign/micro-calcification - malignant)3. Shape (wider than taller - benign / taller than wider - malignant) 4. Margin (well define regular margins - benign/ irregular margins - malignant)5. Echogenicity (anechoic or hyperechoic - benign/ hypoechoic - malignant) Extra edge : Repoing Used for Based on BI-RADS Breast Imaging-Repoing and Data System Breast lesions USG MRI Mammography TI-RADS Thyroid Imaging-Repoing and Data System Thyroid nodules Only based on USG PI-RADS Prostate Imaging-Repoing and Data System Prostate lesions Multi-parametric MRI - T2 weighted imaging - Diffusion weighed imaging - Dynamic contrast enhancement

9923dc78-d422-46d1-b3c7-17e8b28ef589.exp: Answer is A (Frontal lobe abscess) : Development of headache, fever and papilledema in the setting of sinusitis suggest an infective pathology causing raised introcranial tension. Fuher, presence of personality changes suggest a mass lesion affecting the frontal lobe. A brain abscess typically presents as an expanding intracranial mass lesion, and the presence of personality changes limits such a mass lesion to the frontal lobe. A frontal lobe abscess thus explains the presence of an infectious mass lesion causing personality changes.

d2a2e545-7c0d-4e20-8e98-5a68e67bd2f4.exp: Ans. C: Syncytiotrophoblast Human chorionic gonadotropin or human chorionic gonadotrophin (hCG) is a glycoprotein hormone produced in pregnancy that is made by the developing embryo after conception and later by the syncytiotrophoblast (pa of the placenta). Its role is to prevent the disintegration of the corpus luteum of the ovary and thereby maintain progesterone production that is critical for a pregnancy in humans. Early pregnancy testing, in general, is based on the detection or measurement of hCG. Because hCG is produced also by some kinds of tumor, hCG is an impoant tumor marker especially (with clinical significance) in Gestational trophoblastic disease.

f076d80e-fd71-47e5-b74b-288d57b8d7d6.exp:
Some important points regarding spermatogenesis:

Sperms are produced in: seminiferous tubules in testes.
Sertoli cells nurture the developing sperms.
Time for spermatogenesis: 64 days (Thymidine labelling records say it is 74 days).
Normal sperm count: 20 million per ml or 40 million per ejaculate.
Infertility is considered with a sperm count of less than 15 million sperms/ml.
Fructose is the main source of energy for sperms.
Prostaglandins suppress immune response against sperms in the female reproductive tract.
Zinc serves to help to stabilize the DNA-containing chromatin in the sperm cells.
Acrosomal cap houses the various enzymes needed to penetrate the zona pellucida of the oocyte.

1fdfeaea-ed97-44d7-8d66-3cc077f01fb6.exp: Ans. is 'c' i.e., 10 ls Polyvalent antisnake venom is useful when given within four hours of snake bite. It is of less value if delayed for eight hours and is of doubtful value after 24 hours. Each l of polyvalent antisnake venom neutralize about 6-8 mg of venom. Initial dose depends upon symptoms : Minimal (local swelling, no systemic symptoms) --) 5 vails. Moderate (swelling progressing beyond site of bite with systemic reaction)- 10 vails. Severe (markeld local reaction, severe systemic symptoms) - 10-15 vails. In neurotoxic poisoning, a second dose of 10 ls should be given after 1 hour.

0a5543f5-10ff-4248-bcae-594e42cfd5c6.exp: Renal isotope scans such as DTPA, DMSA and MAG3 scan give information regarding renal function. Renal isotope scan can determine if there is obstruction and provide an estimate of differential and total function of the kidneys. Different types of renal scan includes 1. DMSA scan which demonstrate the amount of functional renal coical tissue. 2. DTPA scan demonstrate overall function and obstruction. MAG3 scan can be used in the first few months of life when renal function is low and DTPA scan is ineffective. Diethylene triamine penta acetic acid (DTPA) scan is used to study the functioning status of the kidneys. In this scan gamma camera can measure the rate of excretion of isotope by each kidney and confirm functional obstruction. DMSA scan can show functional renal tissue in detail and may be useful in confirming the presence of scars in the renal coex that may occur in reflux nephropathy. Ref: Jones' Clinical Paediatric Surgery : Diagnosis and Management By Peter G. Jones, John M. Hutson, Alan A Woodward, Page 215; Vital CKD By Rob Higgins, Page 20; Spinal Cord Medicine : Principles and Practice By Vernon W. Lin, Christopher M. Bono, Brunner and Suddah's textbook of Medical-Surgical Nursing By Lillian Sholtis Brunner, Page 1308; Genitourinary Radiology: The Requisites By Ronald J. Zagoria, Page 382

50292b15-05d0-47e2-a85c-727be0403863.exp: Ans. is 'b' i.e., BupropionMedications used in nicotine withdraw!o The most effective medications for smoking cessation includs nicotine replacement therapy. Bupropion and varenicilineMost effective medications for smoking cessationNicotine replacementBupropionVareniclineo Transdermal nicotine patch o Nicotine gum o Nicotine lozenge o Nicotine inhaler o Nicotine nasal spray Nicotine replacement therapyo The goal of nicotine replacement therapy is to provide nicotine to a smoker without using tobacco, thereby relieving nicotine withdraw l symptoms as the smoker breaks the behaviour of cigarette smoking.o The use of nicotine replacement therapy in place of cigarette avoids exposure to carbon monoxide that reduces oxygen delivery to oxidant gases that are atherogenic and to tars that are carcinogenic.There are different types of nicotine replacement products e.g.Nicotine patch - transdermallyNasal spray - nasal mucosaNicotine chewing gum, lozenge or inhaler - oral mucosao Nicotine replacement products differ in their pharmacokinetics and delivery of nicotine to circulation# But no product delivers nicotine as rapidely as cigarette smoking, a factor that contributes specifically to dependence producing properties of cigarettes.Bupropiono It is believed to act by enhancing central nervous system noradrenergic and dopaminergic release,o It is given as sustained release formulation.o The sustained release bupropion takes five to seven days to reach steady state blood levels. Therefore it is started one week before a smoker s target quit date,o Bupropion monotherapy doubles the likelihood of smoking cessation compared to placebo.Varenicilineo Vareniciline is a partial agonist at the alpha-4 beta-2 subunit of nicotinic acetylcholine receptor. The receptor that appears to produce the reinforcing effects of nicotine and leads to nicotine dependence.It aids smoking cessation in two ways# Firstly, as a partial agonist it binds to and produces the partial stimulation of the alpha 4 beta 2 nicotinic receptor thereby reducing the symptoms of nicotine withdrawl.# Secondly since vareniciline binds to the alpha 4 beta 2 receptor subunit with high affinity it blocks the nicotine in tobacco smoking from binding to the receptor.; there ????
